interpretation: ELMSAN1, also known as MIDEAS, is involved in transcription regulation and is part of the mitotic deacetylase complex, which plays a role in cellular processes such as cell cycle regulation and gene expression. While there is no direct evidence linking ELMSAN1 to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), its involvement in metabolic processes and potential roles in cellular stress responses may suggest a connection to the pathophysiology of SONFH, particularly in the context of steroid-induced cellular damage. The evidence retrieved is limited and does not establish a clear mechanistic link to SONFH, leading to a low confidence in its relevance as a biomarker. Therefore, while ELMSAN1 may have biological implications in related pathways, its potential as a clinical biomarker for early detection of SONFH is weak and speculative.
relevance_summary: ELMSAN1 may be involved in metabolic processes that could relate to the pathophysiology of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), but direct evidence is lacking.

Disease: breast cancer

result.title: MIDEAS — mitotic deacetylase associated SANT domain protein
result.text:
Official symbol: MIDEAS
Gene name: mitotic deacetylase associated SANT domain protein
Aliases / synonyms: C14orf117, C14orf43, ELMSAN1, LSR68, c14_5541
Organism: Homo sapiens
Gene summary: Predicted to enable transcription corepressor activity. Predicted to be involved in negative regulation of DNA-templated transcription and regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II. Located in nucleoplasm. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Jul 2025]
result.gene_description: mitotic deacetylase associated SANT domain protein

result.title: LOC132090872 — Neanderthal introgressed variant-containing enhancer experimental_36600
result.text:
Official symbol: LOC132090872
Gene name: Neanderthal introgressed variant-containing enhancer experimental_36600
Organism: Homo sapiens
Gene summary: This genomic region contains a Neanderthal adaptively introgressed expression-modulating variant (emVar). It was validated as a functional enhancer by massively parallel reporter assays (MPRAs) in K562 erythroleukemia cells, with activity observed for both the introgressed and non-introgressed 14:74209097 or rs11624425 variant alleles, and with lower activity for the introgressed rs11624425 'G' variant. CRISPR/Cas9-mediated deletion of a 22 bp subregion centered on rs11624425 indicates that this region positively regulates expression of the MIDEAS (mitotic deacetylase associated SANT domain protein, also known as ELMSAN1) and DNAL1 (dynein axonemal light chain 1) genes. [provided by RefSeq, Sep 2023]

result.title: Loss of Elmsan1 in Cardiomyocytes Leads to Age-Dependent Cardiac Dysfunction and Reduced Lifespan
result.text:
Title: Loss of Elmsan1 in Cardiomyocytes Leads to Age-Dependent Cardiac Dysfunction and Reduced Lifespan
Accession: GSE301440
Study type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
Organism: Mus musculus
Samples: IntegerElement(8, attributes={})
Summary: Histone deacetylase (HDAC) complexes regulate pathological gene programs during heart disease progression. The recently identified mitotic deacetylase complex (MiDAC), which includes DNTTIP1, ELMSAN1, and HDAC1/2, remains the least characterized among these complexes. ELMSAN1 has been implicated in left ventricular remodeling, and its global deletion in mice leads to heart malformation. To investigate its role in mouse heart, we generated cardiomyocyte-specific Elmsan1 knockout (ELM cKO) using αMHC driven Cre recombinase. We analyzed both male and female animals across three experimental group...

Summary: Developing strategies to enhance the response to bromodomain and extraterminal domain (BET) inhibitors and effectively eradicate cancer stem cells would represent a major cancer treatment advance against leukemia. Through a functional CRISPR screen, we identified the aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AHR), a ligand-activated transcription factor, as a critical regulator of MYC expression and BET inhibitor sensitivity in human acute myeloid leukemia (AML). Constitutive or pharmacological activation of AHR repressed MYC and synergized with BET inhibitors to inhibit MYC transcription and suppress leukem...
